assertions.evidence: YAML frontmatter contains 'applyTo' field scoped to TypeScript files

assertions.assertion: All rules are imperative and verifiable
Rule count is concise (under 20 lines of rules)
assertions.evidence: Rules section has 20 or fewer bullet points or statements

assertions.assertion: Skill rejects the vague rule as unverifiable
assertions.evidence: Output explicitly identifies 'write good, clean code' as too vague

assertions.assertion: Skill asks what specific behavior is intended
assertions.evidence: Output asks one or more clarifying questions about concrete expectations

assertions.assertion: Skill offers concrete rewrites as options
assertions.evidence: Output proposes at least one verifiable rule (e.g., max function length, naming pattern)

assertions.assertion: Original vague rule is not emitted unchanged
assertions.evidence: Output does not contain 'write good, clean code' as a final instruction rule

assertions.assertion: Skill checks for or asks about existing conflicting instructions
assertions.evidence: Output asks about existing rules before adding the new one

assertions.assertion: Contradiction is flagged if conflicting rule exists
assertions.evidence: If context shows 'named exports only', output flags the conflict explicitly

assertions.assertion: User is asked to confirm which rule wins
assertions.evidence: Output asks which rule should take precedence before writing

assertions.assertion: Contradicting rule is not silently added
assertions.evidence: Output does not emit a final instruction file with 'default exports' without resolution
